sections.title: ΓÜû∩╕Å Core Principles of General Insurance

cards.title: Indemnity & Subrogation
cards.content:
ΓÇó Indemnity: The purpose of insurance is to restore the insured to the exact financial position before the loss. NO PROFIT can be made.
ΓÇó Subrogation: Once the claim is paid, the insurer steps into the shoes of the insured to recover the loss from the guilty third party.

cards.title: Utmost Good Faith (Uberrima Fides)
cards.content: Both parties must disclose ALL material facts. A material fact is one that affects the insurer's decision to accept the risk or fix the premium.

items.text: If you have 2 policies for the same property, both insurers will share the loss proportionately. You cannot claim full amounts from both.
Causa Proxima: The active, efficient, and direct cause that sets a chain of events in motion leading to the loss.
Mathematical Principle: Sharing the losses of a few among the many who are exposed to similar risks.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Insurable Interest in General Insurance must exist BOTH at the time of taking the policy AND at the time of the loss.
Physical Hazard: A structural defect (e.g., storing petrol in a wooden house).
Moral Hazard: Intentional acts (e.g., setting your own house on fire to claim money).
A risk is insurable only if it is unforeseen, accidental, and the loss can be measured in money.
Personal Accident policies are NOT strictly Contracts of Indemnity, as human life cannot be valued exactly.
